sections.understanding:
CRDB Bank Plc, Tanzania's largest bank with a TZS 10 trillion loan portfolio and 25% market share, is seeking an Expression of Interest for a robust Trade Finance solution to be deployed across its operations in Tanzania, Burundi, and the Democratic Republic of Congo. The EOI is the first gate in a two-stage process — shortlisted vendors will subsequently be invited to respond to a formal Request for Proposal (RFP). This is an international competitive bidding process, meaning ZIMA will compete against global Trade Finance software vendors and large system integrators.

The functional scope demanded is comprehensive and covers the full trade finance lifecycle: (1) customer-facing digital application of trade products such as Letters of Credit and Guarantees, including submission of supporting documentation; (2) automated generation of LC and Guarantee drafts directly from submitted applications; (3) amendment and cancellation workflows for LCs and Guarantees; (4) electronic submission of commercial documents for Export LCs; (5) real-time Trade Finance limits and exposure monitoring at both transaction and customer level; (6) digital review of commercial documents against LC terms — a compliance-critical function; (7) milestone and lifecycle tracking with real-time status visibility; (8) structured end-to-end workflow automation; (9) reporting and analytics; (10) role-based user access and security controls; (11) secure in-system messaging and interactive communication; and (12) full data privacy and protection compliance.

Beyond the functional features, CRDB Bank requires deep integration capability on two fronts: internal integration with the bank's Core Banking System to synchronize customer data, account balances, limits, and transaction postings; and external integration with third-party systems including Supply Chain platforms used by corporate clients and Screening systems for sanctions/AML compliance — a regulatory non-negotiable for international trade finance.

The eligibility criteria open the door to OEMs, system integrators, value-added resellers, and software development companies. Critically, CRDB explicitly allows — and encourages — joint ventures and associations to strengthen value propositions. This is the strategic entry point for ZIMA: partnering with an established Trade Finance solution OEM as the local implementation and integration specialist, providing the in-country expertise, Core Banking System integration know-how, and BoT regulatory integration capability that international vendors typically lack.

The EOI requires specific mandatory deliverables: company profile using templates A.1–A.5, firm experience references using template B, an approach and methodology document (capped at 6 pages), a certificate of incorporation, a valid tax compliance certificate, and audited financial statements for 2023, 2024, and 2025. The submission deadline is 31 March 2026 at 5pm EAT, with the registration-of-interest deadline having been 13 March 2026 — ZIMA should confirm with CRDB procurement whether late registration can be accommodated given the circumstances.
sections.why_zima:
ZIMA Solutions Limited brings a uniquely relevant combination of deep Tanzanian banking technology expertise and enterprise system integration experience that makes it an ideal local implementation and integration partner for this Trade Finance solution initiative. ZIMA has delivered 4 active Bank of Tanzania integrations — RTGS, TIPS, GePG, and NIDA — all running at 99.9% uptime. This is directly transferable to Trade Finance: connecting a new Trade Finance platform to CRDB's Core Banking System, wiring up SWIFT/BoT settlement rails, and integrating AML/sanctions screening systems are precisely the integration patterns ZIMA has mastered in production banking environments.

ZIMA's Core Banking System implementation experience for banks, SACCOs, and microfinance institutions means the team understands banking data models, transaction posting logic, customer limit structures, and the regulatory reporting requirements that a Trade Finance solution must interact with. When CRDB requires that the Trade Finance solution integrate with its CBS to provide real-time limit and exposure monitoring — one of the key functional requirements listed in section 2.1 — ZIMA can architect and deliver that integration with authority, not as a theoretical exercise.

For this EOI, ZIMA proposes to enter as the local system integrator in joint venture with a globally proven Trade Finance solution OEM. This structure gives CRDB Bank the best of both worlds: a battle-tested international Trade Finance platform with deep product capability (LC processing, Guarantee management, SWIFT messaging, document checking workflows) combined with a local Tanzanian partner who knows the regulatory environment, speaks BoT integration language, and can provide responsive on-the-ground support across CRDB's Tanzania, Burundi, and DRC footprint. ZIMA's API Gateway and middleware capability is the glue that will bind the Trade Finance solution to the CBS, screening systems, and supply chain platforms specified in sections 2.1(ii) and 2.1(iii).

ZIMA's AI and analytics capability directly addresses CRDB's reporting and analytics requirement and can extend the Trade Finance platform with predictive risk scoring, trade finance fraud detection, and customer behavioral analytics — capabilities that go beyond what standard Trade Finance products offer out-of-the-box and directly support CRDB's stated goal of enhancing competitive advantage through cutting-edge technology.
approach: Phase 1: EOI Preparation and OEM Partnership Formation (Weeks 1–2). ZIMA will finalize the joint venture agreement with the selected Trade Finance solution OEM, complete all EOI templates (A.1–A.5, B), prepare the approach and methodology document (within the prescribed 6-page limit), and assemble all mandatory attachments including certificate of incorporation, TRA tax compliance certificate, and available financial documentation. ZIMA will register joint venture details with CRDB via tenders@crdbbank.co.tz and ensure submission reaches CRDB by 31 March 2026 at 5pm EAT. Deliverables: Complete EOI submission package in PDF format.

Phase 2: RFP Response and Solution Design (Post-EOI shortlisting, Weeks 1–6). Upon invitation to the RFP process, ZIMA and its OEM partner will conduct detailed requirements workshops with CRDB stakeholders covering trade finance operations, CBS architecture, screening system specifications, and regulatory reporting needs. A detailed solution design document will be produced covering: solution architecture, integration blueprint (CBS connector, SWIFT integration, screening API), data migration strategy, user access model, and security architecture. A sandbox/demo environment will be provisioned for CRDB's evaluation team. Deliverables: Detailed requirements specification, solution architecture document, integration design document, sandbox access credentials.

Phase 3: Implementation and Integration (Months 3–7 post contract award). Core Trade Finance platform deployment in CRDB's infrastructure (cloud or on-premise per CRDB's preference). Phased module rollout: first LC issuance and Guarantee workflows, then commercial document processing and digital document checking, then Export LC and electronic document submission, then real-time limits monitoring and exposure dashboards. Concurrent CBS integration development using ZIMA's proven API Gateway middleware, connecting to CRDB's Core Banking System for customer data, account balances, limit synchronization, and transaction postings. Integration with the bank's screening system for sanctions and AML checks on trade parties. Stage-gates: User Acceptance Testing (UAT) sign-off at each module phase before proceeding. Deliverables: Deployed and integrated Trade Finance modules, UAT sign-off documents, integration test reports.

Phase 4: Testing, Training, and Commissioning (Months 7–9). End-to-end system integration testing (SIT) across all modules and integration points. Performance and load testing to validate system behavior under CRDB's transaction volumes across Tanzania, Burundi, and DRC. Security penetration testing and vulnerability assessment. Technical training for CRDB IT team covering system administration, integration maintenance, and monitoring. User training for Trade Finance operations teams across all branches and subsidiaries. ZIMA will deploy AI-powered analytics dashboards on top of the Trade Finance data for reporting, anomaly detection, and operational insights. Deliverables: SIT and performance test reports, pen test report, training completion certificates, live production environment.

Phase 5: Go-Live Support and Hypercare (Months 9–12). Parallel run of new Trade Finance system alongside existing processes for 30 days to validate accuracy and completeness. Hypercare support with dedicated ZIMA engineers on-site or on standby for 90 days post go-live. Incident management and resolution SLAs agreed upfront. Transition to steady-state support model with defined SLAs for CRDB Tanzania and its Burundi and DRC subsidiaries. Deliverables: Go-live sign-off certificate, hypercare incident log and resolution report, steady-state support agreement, operational runbooks.
sections.value_proposition:
ZIMA's most distinctive value to CRDB Bank is the combination of proven Tanzanian banking infrastructure expertise and the ability to deliver a fully localized implementation — not a remote, offshore engagement. ZIMA's four active Bank of Tanzania integrations (RTGS, TIPS, GePG, NIDA) demonstrate a track record that very few local technology companies can match: production-grade, high-availability banking integrations built to BoT specifications and regulatory standards. For a Trade Finance solution that must interact with settlement rails, correspondent banking, and regulatory reporting, this in-country integration expertise reduces implementation risk significantly compared to engaging an international vendor without local banking infrastructure knowledge.

ZIMA's AI and Machine Learning capability creates a differentiated value layer above the core Trade Finance platform. CRDB's stated goal is not just system replacement but 'securing the bank's future and enhancing competitive advantage.' ZIMA proposes to embed predictive analytics for trade finance risk scoring, AI-driven document compliance checking (flagging discrepancies between presented documents and LC terms), and real-time exposure dashboards that give CRDB's treasury and trade finance teams decision-grade intelligence, not just data. These capabilities are built on ZIMA's existing conversational AI, fraud detection, and credit scoring frameworks, repurposed and fine-tuned for the trade finance domain.

For CRDB's multi-country operations spanning Tanzania, Burundi, and DRC, ZIMA's partnership model ensures regional coverage without the overhead of a large international consultancy. ZIMA will coordinate country-specific configuration, regulatory adaptation, and local user training through its network of technology partners in East and Central Africa, ensuring that the Trade Finance solution serves all three jurisdictions effectively while CRDB maintains a single accountable local point of contact.
compliance_notes: Eligibility — System Integrator / Software Development Company: ZIMA meets the eligibility criteria as a software development company and system integrator. ZIMA proposes to associate with a Trade Finance solution OEM (joint venture), as explicitly permitted under section 2.2, to bring a proven product alongside ZIMA's integration and local delivery capability.

Registration of Interest Deadline (13 March 2026): The registration deadline of 13 March 2026 has passed as of the date of this response (16 March 2026). ZIMA must urgently contact tenders@crdbbank.co.tz to request CRDB's guidance on late registration and whether an exception applies, given that the EOI submission deadline remains 31 March 2026.

Required Solution Features (Section 2.1): All 12 functional capabilities listed in section 2.1 are addressed through the proposed Trade Finance OEM platform (LC/Guarantee workflows, electronic document submission, real-time limits monitoring, document checking, lifecycle tracking, workflow automation, reporting, access control, secure messaging, data privacy) combined with ZIMA's integration capability for CBS and third-party system connectivity.

CBS Integration (Section 2.1.ii): ZIMA's core competency. Addressed via ZIMA's API Gateway middleware, which has delivered 4 production BoT integrations and CBS implementations for financial institutions. Full integration design to be detailed in the RFP response phase.

Third-Party System Integration — Supply Chain and Screening (Section 2.1.iii): ZIMA's API Gateway framework supports REST, SOAP, ISO 20022, SWIFT MT/MX, and custom protocol adapters. Supply chain platform and AML/sanctions screening integrations will be scoped and architected during Phase 2 requirements workshops.

Audited Financial Statements (2023, 2024, 2025): ZIMA was incorporated January 2025. Audited financials for 2023 and 2024 do not exist; 2025 audited accounts will be available May 2026. This is a known gap. ZIMA will address this transparently in the EOI, presenting management accounts, bank statements, and project revenue evidence for 2025, and will include the OEM partner's audited financials to demonstrate the combined financial capacity of the joint venture.

Approach and Methodology (Max 6 Pages, No Template): ZIMA's methodology document will be prepared within the 6-page limit as specified in section 2.4.2, covering each required element: solution feature mapping to section 2.1 requirements, project plan/schedule, phase-by-phase activities, stage-gates, training plan, and phase deliverables, with clearly stated assumptions.

Prescribed Template Format and PDF Submission: All EOI response sections will be presented using CRDB's prescribed Appendix templates (A.1, A.2, A.3, A.4, A.5, B) and the full submission will be compiled and submitted as a single PDF (or split across multiple emails / cloud link if exceeding 10MB) to tenders@crdbbank.co.tz by 31 March 2026 at 5pm EAT.
Certificate of Incorporation: ZIMA Solutions Limited certificate of incorporation (January 2025, Tanzania) will be attached.
Tax Compliance Certificate: Valid TRA tax compliance certificate (TIN: 181-314-605) will be attached.
sections.technical_approach:
ZIMA's technical approach centers on a three-layer architecture: the Trade Finance application layer (provided by the OEM partner), the integration middleware layer (delivered by ZIMA using its proven API Gateway framework), and the analytics and AI layer (delivered by ZIMA on top of trade finance data). The OEM platform handles the domain-specific Trade Finance logic — SWIFT messaging standards (MT700/MT710/MT760 for LCs and Guarantees, MT798 for corporate-to-bank trade), ICC UCP 600 and URDG 758 rule engines, document examination workflows, and customer portal functionality. ZIMA's middleware handles all integration touchpoints: a bi-directional CBS connector for customer master data, account balance inquiry, limit utilization posting, and transaction recording; a screening service adapter for real-time sanctions and AML checks against OFAC, UN, and local BoT watchlists; and a supply chain system API bridge for buyer/seller document exchange and trade event notifications.

For the digital document checking requirement — one of the most technically demanding items in section 2.1 — ZIMA proposes to augment the OEM's rule-based document examination engine with ZIMA's AI document analysis capability. Using optical character recognition (OCR) and natural language processing trained on ICC standard document formats, the system will flag discrepancies between presented shipping documents, bills of lading, commercial invoices, and the terms stipulated in the LC — reducing manual review time and compliance risk for CRDB's trade finance officers. This AI layer is built on ZIMA's existing machine learning infrastructure and can be deployed as a microservice sitting alongside the core Trade Finance platform.

Security architecture will follow a defense-in-depth model: TLS 1.3 encryption for all data in transit, AES-256 for data at rest, multi-factor authentication for all user access, role-based access control (RBAC) with separation of duties for critical trade finance workflows (application, approval, settlement), full audit trails for every transaction and document action, and regular penetration testing cadence. ZIMA will align the security architecture to Bank of Tanzania cybersecurity guidelines and CRDB's existing information security policies. For CRDB's multi-country operations, data residency requirements for Burundi and DRC will be assessed during Phase 2 and addressed through jurisdiction-specific deployment configurations.
sections.risk_management:
The primary risk in this engagement is integration complexity — connecting the Trade Finance solution to CRDB's Core Banking System, screening systems, and supply chain platforms across three countries with potentially different CBS instances or configurations. ZIMA mitigates this risk through its proven integration methodology: beginning with a detailed integration discovery workshop in Phase 2 before any development starts, building against a formal integration specification document approved by CRDB's IT architecture team, and deploying integrations incrementally with stage-gate sign-offs rather than attempting a big-bang cutover. ZIMA's four live BoT integrations demonstrate that this methodology produces reliable, production-grade results in the Tanzanian banking context. A sandbox/testing environment will be maintained throughout the project to allow CRDB's team to validate integration behavior before each stage-gate, minimizing the risk of surprises at go-live.

The secondary risk is change management and user adoption across CRDB's distributed branch network and multi-country subsidiaries. Trade finance is a specialist, high-value business line where operational errors carry significant financial and reputational consequences. ZIMA will mitigate adoption risk through a structured training program that includes both technical training for IT teams and hands-on operational training for trade finance officers, supported by detailed user guides and quick-reference materials tailored to CRDB's specific workflows. The 30-day parallel run in Phase 5 provides a safety net, ensuring that no live trade transactions are processed solely on the new system until the operations team has validated accuracy and completeness. ZIMA's hypercare support model — with dedicated engineers on standby for 90 days post go-live — ensures that any issues surfacing in early production are resolved rapidly, protecting CRDB's service levels and customer commitments during the critical transition period.
